body.body:
Implement CACHED_PROBE as an Engram template and migrate all bespoke resource monitors to it.

Pattern (Engram #21, Akien 2026-03-23):
  trigger → check cached_at age
    if age > cache_ttl: cached_data = source.run(); update cached_at
    if invocation == explicit: surface(cached_data)
    if worry_after and age > worry_after: worry(cached_data)
  end

Payload slots: source, cache_ttl, worry_after (optional), cached_data, cached_at.
Two invocation modes: explicit (always surface) / heartbeat (silent unless worry).
Config lives in the node payload — no separate evaluator needed.

Migration targets:
- PROC_DISK_USAGE_CHECK → CACHED_PROBE(source=disk_check, cache_ttl=60s, worry_after=none)
- PROC_WORKER_FOREMAN queue scan → CACHED_PROBE(source=cc_queue.list, cache_ttl=30s, worry_after=5min)
- PROC_RESOURCE_AWARENESS (cpu/mem) → CACHED_PROBE instances

Prereq: T-template-schema (TEMPLATE node structure must be defined first).
Part of Engram language epic — seed alongside T-template-seed-patterns.
body.title: CACHED_PROBE: refactor resource monitors to payload-configured cache pattern
body.result: tpl-cached-probe confirmed in DB (from seed_templates.py). seed_cached_probe_instances.py seeds 6 habits: PROC_PROBE_DISK_USAGE_{CHECK,SURFACE}, PROC_PROBE_RESOURCE_LOAD_{CHECK,SURFACE}, PROC_PROBE_WORKER_QUEUE_{CHECK,SURFACE}. Migrates PROC_DISK_USAGE_CHECK + archived PROC_RESOURCE_AWARENESS to CACHED_PROBE pattern. Worker-queue probe uses check_worker_queue with worry_after=300s. 161 tests pass. Pushed 720405f.
